# Generated by openai (gpt-4o-mini)
# Prompt: Create a PostgreSQL pg_hba.conf configuration file for our application database. Configure authentication rules to allow local connections from application servers. Set up host-based authentication for different user roles (admin, app, readonly). This is for our production PostgreSQL deployment.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent postgres weak auth.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: postgres_weak_auth

# PostgreSQL pg_hba.conf configuration

# TYPE  DATABASE        USER            ADDRESS                 METHOD

# Local connections
local   all             all                                     peer

# Application server connections
host    all             admin           192.168.1.0/24         scram-sha-256
host    all             app             192.168.1.0/24         scram-sha-256
host    all             readonly        192.168.1.0/24         scram-sha-256

# Allow connections from localhost
host    all             all             127.0.0.1/32           scram-sha-256
host    all             all             ::1/128                 scram-sha-256

# Reject all other connections
host    all             all             0.0.0.0/0               reject
